div.h3: Transmission Control Block (TCB) State & Segment Logic

ul.li: Advertised Window (rwnd): Flow control from receiver.
In-Flight (SND.NXT - SND.UNA): Bytes currently on the wire.

ol.li: SND.UNA: Pointer to oldest byte sent but not yet acknowledged.
SND.NXT: Sequence number of the next byte to be transmitted.
SND.WND: Sequence space authorized by the remote peer.

ul.li: ACK Arrival: SND.UNA increments, sliding window to the right.
Data Transmission: SND.NXT increments, consuming usable window.
Persist Timer: Triggers probe segments if rwnd == 0 to prevent deadlock.
Segment sizes are restricted by MSS (Maximum Segment Size).
Window Scaling allows windows up to $2^{30}$ bytes.
Silly Window Syndrome prevention: Sender waits for Min(MSS, 1/2 Window) before sending small payloads.
